reasoning: Unsafe findings are false positives; flagged scripts are application-level components not executed during AUR build/install. Critical files (PKGBUILD, install hooks) show no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged application runtime scripts (copilotCLIShim.js, copilotCLITodoWorker.js) as build-time risks. Deepseek's inconclusive was due to token limits on irrelevant files.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to prioritize build-critical files over application code (e.g., ignored possible post-install hooks in .install)
Qwen skipped reviewing git-editor.sh/ssh-askpass.sh despite inclusion in file list
false_positives: copilotCLIShim.js: 'curl|bash' pattern is for Copilot CLI install during VS Code runtime, not AUR build
copilotCLITodoWorker.js: SQLite access is extension functionality, not executed during package installation
learnings: File selection: Prioritize PKGBUILD, .install, and referenced scripts over application source code. Suppress files >1MB unless directly executed during build.
Prompt quality: Explicitly instruct models to ignore application runtime behavior unless triggered by PKGBUILD/.install hooks.
Failure modes: Flagging 'curl|bash' in application code is common FP; add context that IDE extensions are out-of-scope unless build-triggered.
